test.name: Resolving a genuine identity conflict should route to conflict-resolution
test.description: User has two genuinely competing candidate persons and wants the conflict adjudicated — which of two different people is the real subject. That is conflict-resolution (resolve a genuine identity conflict where multiple candidate persons compete), per person-evidence's own 'Do NOT use when' clause. person-evidence assigns an assertion to a person during linking; it does not adjudicate a standing conflict between two competing identities. This is the nearest neighbor (competing identities vs. same-identity linking), so it is the most important boundary to hold.

input.user_message: Two different Patrick Flynns are competing for this record — sort out which one is really our subject and resolve the conflict.

negative.explanation: Adjudicating a genuine conflict between two competing candidate persons is conflict-resolution. person-evidence creates a revisable link from an assertion to a person; it does not resolve a standing identity conflict. The skill's own description excludes this case ('resolve a genuine identity conflict where multiple candidate persons compete (use conflict-resolution)').

judge_context: Should route to conflict-resolution — the user wants a standing conflict between two competing candidate persons adjudicated, which person-evidence's description explicitly excludes
Linking an assertion to a person (person-evidence) is not the same as resolving which of two competing people is real (conflict-resolution); declining and redirecting is the correct behavior
